title: hypochaeris glabra subsp. minima
intro: Hypochaeris glabra subsp. minima, also known as the small smooth cat's ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is a small, annual herb with leaves that are smooth and hairless, and yellow flowers that bloom in clusters. Hypochaeris glabra subsp. minima has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high levels of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is said to provide health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function.
